2003-12-01.title: India Plans Free AIDS Therapy, but Effort Hinges on Price Accord With Drug Makers
2003-12-01.abstract: Government officials announce that India will expand substantially its program to provide antiretroviral therapy to people with HIV and AIDS; say free therapy will begin by Apr of 2004 to all HIV-positive new parents and all children under 15, in six states with highest rates of HIV and AIDS; say eventually all people in those states with full-blown AIDS are to be treated; decision amounts to significant policy shift for India, but several obstacles must be overcome; government has not yet reached final agreement with country's pharmaceutical companies, which manufacture generic versions of antiretroviral medications, to reduce their prices, and has yet to identify 'budgetary support' needed to pay for drugs; photo (M)

2003-12-01.title: For the Iraqis, a Missile Deal That Went Sour; Files Tell of Talks With North Korea
2003-12-01.abstract: Bush administration officials say computer files found in Iraq by international inspectors show Saddam Hussein's sons, generals and front companies were engaged in lengthy negotiations with North Korea for two years before American invasion of Iraq; say they believe goal was to obtain full production line to manufacture, under Iraqi flag, North Korean missile system, which would be capable of hitting American allies and bases around region; say negotiations were mostly conducted in Syria, apparently with knowledge of Syrian government; say Hussein discovered that North Korean leader Kim Jong Il is less than fully reliable negotiating partner; say Hussein appears to have gotten nothing in return for his $10 million down payment; trail investigators have uncovered shows Iraqi officials traveled to Syria month before American invasion to demand that North Korea refund $1.9 million because it failed to meet deadlines for delivering its first shipment of goods; trail also shows that Syria was major arms-trading bazaar for Hussein government (M)

2003-12-01.title: Daud and the Pupils of Zhob
2003-12-01.abstract: The Talibans guerilla war against the US army is becoming more violent. The Jihadis often retreat to Pakistan following their attacks. New fighters for the war in Afghanistan are constantly being recruited in the Pashtun tribal regions, while Islamabad looks the other way.

2003-12-01.title: Hartlepool Journal; British Greet Navy Rustbuckets With a Volley of Venom
2003-12-01.abstract: Arrival of two decrepit United States Navy ships sets off protests in Hartlepool, Eng, job-hungry town which for decades has welcomed petrochemical plants, steel and coal companies; ships are waiting, prehaps indefinitely, to be stripped of their toxic innards and recycled for their steel by Able UK, which won $17.8 milion contract to dismantle 13 US ships; residents of Hartlepool are concerned about environmental dangers; their protests have turned nasty; photo; map (M)

2003-12-01.title: Informal Peace Plan for Mideast Is Unveiled in Geneva
2003-12-01.abstract: The unreality of the event was overshadowed by the painful pleas from the negotiators to their leaders to start new peace talks.

2003-12-02.title: World Briefing | Africa: Liberia: Russians To Join Peacekeepers
2003-12-02.abstract: Russian President Vladimir V Putin orders 40 communications specialists and military observers to join United Nations peacekeepers in Liberia (S)

2003-12-02.title: World Briefing: Asia
2003-12-02.abstract: INDONESIA: MilitantS Conviction Reversed; INDIA: Assembly Elections In 4 States; INDIA: Ex-Police Commissioner Arrested;.

2003-12-02.title: World Briefing | Europe: Northern Ireland: Britain Discusses Talks
2003-12-02.abstract: Northern Ireland Secretary of State Paul Murphy talks with Democratic Unionists, who now represent largest party in provincial Assembly; efforts to reopen governing body are not likely in near future as Unionists refuse to work with Sinn Fein, Irish Republican Army's political wing (S)

2003-12-02.title: Rumsfeld Calls Peril in Iraq, Along With Progress, a 'Contradiction'
2003-12-02.abstract: Defense Secretary Donald H. Rumsfeld acknowledged Monday that the security environment in Iraq presented ''a contradiction'' in which attacks from ''a limited number'' of adversaries continued to kill or wound American and allied forces even as schools and hospitals open and the economy stabilized.    Joining Mr. Rumsfeld at a NATO defense ministers meeting here, Gen. Peter Pace, vice chairman of the Joint Chiefs of Staff, spoke of a grim lesson for America's foes that emerged from the ambush on Sunday of an Army convoy in Samarra.

2003-12-02.title: Big Business Plays Largest Role in Current Russian Vote
2003-12-02.abstract: In America the relationship between big business and politics may often be cozy; in Russia it is fast becoming intimate.    The nation's largest businesses, from oil giants to banks to manufacturers, have not only poured money into the parliamentary elections to be held on Sunday, but have also filled party tickets with dozens of their own executives.

2003-12-03.title: Israeli Warns Powell on Peace Team; He Rejects Criticism
2003-12-03.abstract: Israeli Vice premier Ehud Olmert, in rare government criticism of US, says it would be mistake for Sec Colin Powell to meet with Israeli and Palestinian politicians who have negotiated symbolic Mideast peace plan; Powell, in Tunis, says that what self-appointed negotiators are saying is that situation has to change; US overtures are considered way of prodding Sharon government (M)

2003-12-03.title: China Is Romping With the Neighbors (U.S. Is Distracted)
2003-12-03.abstract: Letter From Asia reports China is building better relations with Asian neighbors, capitalizing on US preoccupation with terrorism to greatly expand influence in region; photos; new affinity is promoted by two-way tourism at time when terrorism warnings keep Americans away; China is also on buying spree in Southeast Asian countries where significant Muslim populations make US concentration on terrorism particularly unappealing (M)

2003-12-03.title: After Raids, U.S. Denies Capturing Hussein Aide
2003-12-03.abstract: American forces carry out raids intended to get Izzat Ibrahim, former senior aide to Saddam Hussein believed to be coordinating some of attacks on US troops; officials later deny reports of his capture; more than 1,000 soldiers seal off northern city of Hawija, Sunni enclave in mostly Kurdish area; photo; US soldier is killed by homemade bomb near Samarra (M)

2003-12-03.title: With All That Oil, Baghdad Wonders at Lengthy Gas Lines
2003-12-03.abstract: Motorists in Baghdad have for past two weeks waited for hours to fill gas tanks; officials blame guerrilla attacks on northern oil pipelines (S)

2003-12-03.title: Attacks by Arabs On Jews in France Revive Old Fears
2003-12-03.abstract: Arab attacks on Jews in France and incidents like burning of Jewish school in Gagny intensify debate over definition and extent of anti-Semitism in France and Europe, and force French government to redouble efforts to combat it; photos; French Jews, particularly those of North African origin, say Israeli-Palestinian conflict and US occupation of Iraq have morphed into battleground for Muslims and others in Europe who make no distinction between Israel's political policies and Judaism; France has 600,000 Jewish citizens, largest population outside Israel and US, but perhaps ten times that number of Arab Muslims; unemployed Arab youths who wander streets and carry out attacks have no understanding of Holocaust or Europe's long and continuing history of anti-Semitism, as seen in recent European Union poll and assaults on Jews in several countries (M)

2003-12-04.title: His Dream, to Be the Light at the End of the Israeli-Palestinian Tunnel
2003-12-04.abstract: Faux-peace agreement between Israelis and Palestinians celebrated with much fanfare in Geneva was dreamed up by university professor Alexis Keller and financed with help of his father, banker-philanthropist Pierre Keller; Palestinian negotiator Yasir Abed Rabbo says he was seduced by Keller's 'enlightened amateurism'; Keller recalls proposing to Yossi Beilin that secret talks could finalize Taba negotiations; photo (M)

2003-12-04.title: LUXURY IN A COLD CLIMATE: Brands turn on to senses
2003-12-04.abstract: PARIS—  The luxury world is awash with logos and powerful visual images. But for the 21st century, there is a revolution in store. Let's call it "emotional" branding, the use of scent, touch, sound and even taste in marketing.

2003-12-04.title: Saudi Arsenal Is Found
2003-12-04.abstract: Vast arsenal, including surface-to-air missiles, is found in hands of suspect in bombing of residential compound in Riyadh, Saudi Arabia (S)

2003-12-04.title: Liberias Ex-Leader Is Put on Interpols Most Wanted List
2003-12-04.abstract: Charles G. Taylor has been indicted on war crimes charges by a special tribunal in Sierra Leone.

2003-12-04.title: A Billionaire Wants to Be Moscow's Mayor
2003-12-04.abstract: Billionaire businessman Aleksandr Lebedev wages come-from-behind campaign to become mayor of Moscow; calls for more financial accountability and end to micromanagement and cozy deals of incumbent Yuri Luzhkov (S)

2003-12-05.title: Bomb Kills U.S. Soldier and 2 Iraqis in Baghdad
2003-12-05.abstract: An American soldier and at least two Iraqi civilians were killed today when an improvised bomb exploded on a commercial strip in south Baghdad.

2003-12-05.title: Pentagon and Bogus News: All Is Denied
2003-12-05.abstract: Pentagon has quietly awarded $300,000 contract to SAIC, major defense consultant, to study how Defense Dept could design 'effective strategic influence' campaign to combat global terror; this comes year after Defense Sec Rumsfeld disbanded Pentagon's Office of Strategic Influence after it became known that office was considering plans to provide false news items to unwitting foreign journalists to influence policymakers and public sentiment abroad; senior Pentagon officials say new contract with SAIC does not reflect backdoor effort to resurrect discredited office and is merely study to understand Al Qaeda better and find ways to combat it (M)

2003-12-05.title: A Tale of War: Iraqi Describes Battling G.I.'s
2003-12-05.abstract: American commanders say people fighting them in Hawija, Iraq, seem more brazen recently, even circulating leaflets asking all Iraqis to join them; Iraqi man claiming to be former soldier describes what he says are operations of his cell, which he says consists of 15 men, mostly former soldiers, who take no direct orders from anyone but are in contact with other similar groups; says targets are set by those with more military experience; man's full story cannot be independently corroborated; some details given by him dovetail with comments from American military, although one American officer dismisses man's assertion that his cell has killed total of 500 Americans; says total of six Americans have been killed in area since late Mar; photos; map (M)

2003-12-05.title: Berlusconi Urges Support for U.S. on Iraq
2003-12-05.abstract: Italian Prime Min Silvio Berlusconi urges all European nations to back United Sates efforts in Iraq, wide ranging interview; calls intervention important, necessary example of West exporting freedom; echoes and perhaps goes beyond Pres Bush's doctrine of pre-emptive intervention to head off terrorist threats; trumpets his plans to transform Italy's economy; discusses, without apology, his ever-lengthening string of political gaffes; photo (M)

2003-12-05.title: World Briefing | The Environment: Kremlin Seems To Reverse Itself Again On Kyoto
2003-12-05.abstract: Andrei N Illarionov, top economic adviser to Russian President Vladimir V Putin. reaffirms statement earlier this week that Putin has decided not to ratify Kyoto Protocol; agreement is designed to control emissions linked to global warming and needs Russia's backing to be enacted; United States has refused to ratify treaty (S)

2003-12-06.title: World Briefing | Asia: Philippines: Moratorium On Executions Ends
2003-12-06.abstract: Philippine President Gloria Macapagal Arroyo reinstates death penalty (S)

2003-12-06.title: Roadside Bomb Kills a G.I. And 2 Civilians in Baghdad
2003-12-06.abstract: American soldier and at least two Iraqi civilians are killed when bomb explodes on commercial strip in south Baghdad; about 12 Iraqis are wounded; attack is first fatal blast in Baghdad since Nov 15; photo (M)

2003-12-06.title: Trail of Anti-U.S. Fighters Said to Cross Europe to Iraq
2003-12-06.abstract: European intelligence and law enforcement officials say recent arrests of terror suspects show that Al Qaeda and groups linked to it have established network across Europe that is moving recruits into Iraq to join insurgency against American-led forces; network is in at least six countries and has assisted hundreds of young men, providing fake travel documents, training, money and infiltration routes, and has become better organized in recent months; it is not clear whether foreign terror recruits are playing significant role in insurgency; Pres Bush and administrator L Paul Bremer cite influx of 'jihadists,' but Gen John P Abizaid, regional commander, says numbers are small; details of investigations in Germany and Italy discussed; map (M)

2003-12-06.title: General Gets 20 Years for Sarajevo Atrocities
2003-12-06.abstract: Judges at war crimes tribunal at the Hague sentence former Gen Stanislav Galic to 20 years in prison for his role in siege of Sarajevo; he is convicted of war crimes and crimes against humanity; photo (M)

2003-12-06.title: An Ally of Sharon Foresees a Palestinian State
2003-12-06.abstract: Ehud Olmert, Israel's deputy prime minister, predicts that Israelis and Palestinians will not reach peace agreement, but that Israel will have to cede territory and permit creation of Palestinian state; comments are published in Yediot Ahronot, nation's largest newspaper; Olmert says Israeli Jews risk becoming minority controlling Arab majority, echoing widespread fear that Palestinians will stop calling for two-state solution, instead demanding single state where they outnumber Jews; interview is sharply criticized by other Likud members (M)

2003-12-07.title: Russian Parliament Elections Could Add to Putin's Power
2003-12-07.abstract: United Russia, party supported by Pres Vladimir Putin, appears headed for resounding victory in Russia's parliamentary elections, one big enough to give it outright majority in Duma; Kremlin could hold sway over two-thirds majority, depending on success of smaller parties allied with United Russia; Putin could emerge with something close to absolute power; photo (M)

2003-12-07.title: How a Shady Iranian Deal Maker Kept the Pentagon's Ear
2003-12-07.abstract: Stephen J Hadley, Pres Bush's deputy national security adviser, reportedly raised no objections to clandestine meeting that Pentagon officials held with Iranian dissidents in December 2001 and June 2002, but said he does not remember being told that meetings were initiated by Manucher Ghorbanifar, Iranian who acted as middleman in Iran-contra affair during Reagan administration and was long ago labeled as fabricator by American intelligence officials; Senate committee is seeking to determine whether senior administration officials were aware of Ghorbanifar's involvement in meetings; fresh details about contacts shed line on rift between American intelligence agencies and more hawkish officials at Pentagon and in White House who pursued contacts; photo (M)

2003-12-07.title: To Fill an Empty Far East, Russians Look to Refugees
2003-12-07.abstract: Refugees from North Korea are offered welcome by officials in Russia's Far East, who are alarmed by 17 percent decline in region's population during 1990's; it is unclear whether government in Moscow, which controls immigration policy, will want to risk increasing regional tensions and racial insecurities among Russians in Far East; Refugees International says Russian proposal shows willingness to share some of burden and could be building block for dealing with humanitarian problem of North Korea; photos; map (M)

2003-12-07.title: Macedonian Guilty Of Slavery Charges
2003-12-07.abstract: Macedonian court convicts Dilaver Bojku of enslaving dozens of young women from Ukraine, Romania and Moldova for purpose of prostitution; sentences him to three years and eight months (S)

2003-12-07.title: U.S. Commander in Iraq Expects Attacks to Increase
2003-12-07.abstract: Lt. Gen. Ricardo Sanchez added that insurgent forces were bent on disrupting Iraqs transition to democracy.

2003-12-08.title: U.S. AND 2 ALLIES AGREE ON A PLAN FOR NORTH KOREA
2003-12-08.abstract: Broadly worded set of principles to end North Korea's nuclear program is agreed to by United States, South Korea and Japan; it calls for 'coordinated' set of steps in which five nations would offer North Korea security guarantee as it begins verifiable disassembly of its nuclear facilities; statement is being sent to China's leaders in hopes that Beijing will pass it on to North Korean leader Kim Jong Il; Bush administration and Asian officials say North Korea might object to offer because it sets no timetable for energy or economic aid to country, and because it would require inspections of suspect facilities that have never before been opened; joint position does not demand that North Korea return to Nuclear Nonproliferation Treaty, as called for in past, because American, Chinese and South Korean officials doubt that Kim would agree to re-enter treaty; omission of that demand may fit well with plans of Bush administration hawks, who say movements of inspectors from International Atomic Energy Agency were so restricted in North Korea that their presence was nearly useless; they say administration will insist that North Korean disarmament be verified by teams of American and Asian inspectors, backed by intelligence gathered by satellites and interviews with North Korean defectors (M)

2003-12-08.title: Philippines to punish abductors with death
2003-12-08.abstract: MANILA:—  The execution of condemned criminals, set to restart next month after a three-year moratorium, will apply only to those convicted of kidnapping, the Philippine government said.

2003-12-08.title: Leaving for the Death Zone
2003-12-08.abstract: For years militant Islamists have been leaving Germany for other parts of the world to fight a holy war. Their latest destination is Iraq.

2003-12-08.title: Codename Abu Nidal
2003-12-08.abstract: Outside the empty apartment of one of the fugitives, an "enemy of the state," stand the slippers of his wife and mother, one pair is white, the other green. A neighbor says that Azad Ekinci's entire family disappeared from Bingöl the evening of the attacks on the two synagogues in Istanbul.

2003-12-08.title: RUSSIA'S VOTING FOR PARLIAMENT BOLSTERS PUTIN
2003-12-08.abstract: Russians vote for party loyal to Pres Vladimir V Putin in parliamentary elections, expanding his already dominant political control in first electoral test of his leadership since he became president four years ago; party, United Russia, is alliance of politicians, ministers and businessmen united less by ideology than by their support of Putin; it collects 36.7 percent of vote, more than double that of any other party; Communist Party, which won largest bloc of seats in two previous parliamentary elections, fades badly after bruising campaign that reflects internal divisions, challenges from breakaway parties and relentless assaults on state television; about half of country's eligible voters cast ballots; exact composition of new Parliament might not be known for several days, but results hand Putin strong mandate; what he and United Russia plan to do with that mandate remains unclear; many in Russia and abroad have raised alarms over steady erosion of democratic freedoms, but voters have clearly embraced political stability and economic development that Putin and his party promise to nurture (M)

2003-12-09.title: Bush Tells China Leader He Opposes Taiwans Referendum
2003-12-09.abstract: President Bush bluntly warned Taiwan that he would not tolerate any any attempt to change its relationship with Beijing.

2003-12-09.title: Revenge of Old Europe / Czech policy under fire : Nobility seeks to win back seized property
2003-12-09.abstract: VIENNA—  New Europe is facing a challenge from the most unlikely of sources: Old Europe — extremely Old Europe, that is. Just ask the prince.

2003-12-09.title: U.N. Assembly Puts Off Action on Human Cloning Ban
2003-12-09.abstract: The debate has set the Bush administration against allies like Britain and large parts of the worlds scientific community.

2003-12-09.title: World Briefing | Middle East: Iran: Kidnappers Seize Tourists
2003-12-09.abstract: Iranian Student News Agency reports that kidnappers in Sistan-Baluchestan Province have seized one Irish and two German tourists and are demanding $6 million ransom; area is near Pakistan and Afghanistan border (S)

2003-12-09.title: World Briefing: Europe
2003-12-09.abstract: ITALY: Court Takes On Immunity Issue; THE NETHERLANDS: Heiress To The Throne; NORWAY: King Undergoes Cancer Surgery; BRITAIN: Victory Against Rust Buckets; BRITAIN: Court Upholds 1955 Hanging; GERMANY: Cannibal Defendant Sought Other Victims;.

2003-12-10.title: World Briefing | Europe: Italy: Water Tampering Spreads
2003-12-10.abstract: Thousands of bottles of mineral water are taken from shelves as incidents of tampering are reported in several regions of Italy; nearly dozen people have been hospitalized from bleach and acetone tainted water (S)

2003-12-10.title: World Briefing: Europe
2003-12-10.abstract: SPAIN: Eta Suspect Arrested In France; ITALY: Water Tampering Spreads; GERMANY: 70S Terrorist Freed;.

2003-12-10.title: Bush Lauds China Leader As 'Partner' In Diplomacy
2003-12-10.abstract: Pres Bush welcomes Chinese Prime Min Wen Jiabao to White House, declaring their two countries 'partners in diplomacy'; photo; warns Taiwan against attempting to change its relationship with mainland; leaders discuss China's key role as mediator and conduit of messages with North Korea; Bush rejects North Korea's offer to consider re-freezing its nuclear program in return for right concessions; Wen reportedly says North Korea is not yet ready for multilateral talks; offers few concrete words on China's surging trade surplus with US, suggesting to Bush economic advisers that solution is more US exports rather than curbs on China; Sen Charles Schumer, sponsor of bill for punitive tariffs, says China calculates that Bush will not make issue of trade when he needs Chinese help on North Korea (M)

2003-12-10.title: Seeking Balance: Growth vs. Culture in Amazon
2003-12-10.abstract: Article from Pumpuentsa, Ecuador, on fight by indigenous Indians to fend off international energy companies that are moving into Amazon basin to tap some of last untouched oil and natural gas reserves; tactics of Indians struggling to hold onto nearly primordial way of life range from protests and savvy cooperation with environmentalists to vandalism and kidnapping of industry workers; outcome will determine if Amazon resources become key part of Latin American development and US strategy to diversify energy sources; region already provides more oil to US than Middle East does and governments are trying to lure investors to natural riches along 1,000 miles of forests and Andean foothills, from Colombia to Bolivia and into Brazil's vast interior; photos; map; efforts by environmentalists to hold off exploration in one rain forest region of Ecuador described (L)

2003-12-10.title: Iranian Accepts Nobel Peace Prize, and Criticizes the West
2003-12-10.abstract: Shirin Ebadi declared that the award would inspire women across the Muslim world to fight for equality in oppressive, patriarchal societies.

2003-12-11.title: German Judge Frees 9/11 Suspect, Citing New Evidence
2003-12-11.abstract: The judges decision raised questions about the conviction of another man in the case who was convicted on similar charges and sentenced to 15 years in prison.

2003-12-11.title: A New Prime Minister Is Ready to Take the Reins in Canada
2003-12-11.abstract: Paul Martin takes over as prime minister of Canada after prolonged transition in which he made promises to liberals and conservatives, business and environmental groups, Westerners and urban dwellers; what he actually does believe has become matter of conjecture; he will lead interim govnerment in preparation for national elections in spring; he has many vital social issues to deal with before election, in which he and Liberal Party will face weak opposition parties (M)

2003-12-11.title: U.S. Says Other Afghan Children Died in Earlier Raid
2003-12-11.abstract: American military spokesman acknowledges for second time in week that children were victims of airstrikes aimed at Taliban fighters in Afghanistan; says six children and two adults were found under collapsed wall after attack by American Special Forces on compound of known militant near Gardez; says airstrike was called in after soldiers came under heavy machine-gun fire; United States previously acknowledged having killed nine children in airstrike against suspected Taliban fighter in Hutala; killings are embarrassment to military, which did not acknowledge them for several days, and then only in response to question at news conference; United Nations and office of Pres Hamid Karzai express renewed concerns about political damage inflicted by such incidents; photo; map (M)

2003-12-11.title: French Panel Recommends Banning Head Scarves in Schools
2003-12-11.abstract: A report on church-state relations in France was delivered today against the backdrop of a Europe struggling with the integration of Muslims and rising anti-Semitism.

2003-12-11.title: Allies Angered at Exclusion From Bidding
2003-12-11.abstract: Russia, France, Germany, Canada and other countries barred by Pentagon from bidding for $18.6 billion worth of prime reconstruction contracts in Iraq react angrily, saying move is all the more astonishing given recent appeals by Washington for help in post-war Iraq; Russian Defense Min Sergei B Ivanov quickly rules out any write-off of Iraq's $120 billion debt; photo (M)

2003-12-12.title: Italy steps up hunt for source of bottled-water tampering
2003-12-12.abstract: MILAN:—  Investigators have intensified their efforts to track down the so-called Acquabomber, who for the past month has been injecting small quantities of toxic substances into plastic bottles of mineral water with a syringe, sickening several people.

2003-12-12.title: World Briefing: Europe
2003-12-12.abstract: LITHUANIA: Impeachment Appears Closer; RUSSIA: Kidnapped Boy Freed After Three Years; ITALY: Man Dies In Synagogue Blast; DENMARK: Old Literary Thefts Solved;.

2003-12-12.title: World Briefing | Europe: Italy: Man Dies In Synagogue Blast
2003-12-12.abstract: Car parked next to Italian synagogue explodes and kills Jordanian driver; officials contend event is suicide not terrorism (S)

2003-12-12.title: Taking Supplies to the Troops, 'Coming In High and Fast'
2003-12-12.abstract: Task of sustaining military operations in Iraq and 155,000 American and allied troops there falls to unsung supply lifeline of ground convoys from Kuwait and complex air bridge of cargo flights from United States and Europe; Air Force uses fleets of three Air Force cargo planes, C-130, C-17 and C-5; huge task described; photos; chart (M)

2003-12-12.title: 2 American Journalists Wounded in Iraq
2003-12-12.abstract: Journalists James Nachtwey and Michael Weisskopf, covering Iraq conflict for Time magazine, are wounded in Baghdad when grenade is thrown into Humvee they are riding in while accompanying United States military personnel; are in stable condition (S)

2003-12-13.title: An Indonesian Voice in the Antiterror Wilderness
2003-12-13.abstract: Profile of Gen A M Hendropriyono, head of Indonesia's intelligence agency; Hendro, as he is known, alerted Indonesian leaders after Sept 11 that country was likely terrorist target, but says they ignored him and even denounced him as tool of Americans; says he feels vindicated now that political colleagues can no longer deny that terrorism is problem; has become important component of American campaign against terror and has helped Indonesia arrest scores of militants from Jemaah Islamiyah; photo (M)

2003-12-13.title: World Briefing | Europe: Germany: Section Of Berlin Wall Is Sold
2003-12-13.abstract: Thirty-three yard-long section of Berlin Wall is sold at auction to anonymous buyer for $212,000; sale stipulates wall cannot be moved, painted or torn down; stands on lot near Nordbahnhof (Germany) train station; photo (S)

2003-12-13.title: Bush Sees Need For Repayment If Fee Was High
2003-12-13.abstract: Pres Bush says he expects Halliburton to repay $61 million if subsidiary Kellogg, Brown & Root is found to have overcharged Pentagon on contract to deliver fuel to Iraq; Halliburton charged government $2.27 a gallon for 56.6 million gallons of gas from Kuwait, price that was $1.09 more than another contractor; issue has become lightning rod because of Vice Pres Dick Cheney's connection to Halliburton; Democrats are charging Halliburton's role in Iraq reflects administration's willingness to do favors for friendly corporate interests; Bush also defends his choice of James A Baker III as envoy for talks with other nations about forgiving Iraq's debts; Baker's law firm Baker Botts, and investment firm Carlyle Group, where he is investment counselor, have business interests in Middle East (M)

2003-12-13.title: Iranian Rebels Urge Pentagon Not to Let Iraq Expel Them
2003-12-13.abstract: Representatives of Iranian opposition group People's Mujahedeen, or Mujahedeen Khalq, appeal to Pentagon to overrule order by Iraqi Governing Council that would expel its members from Iraq by end of year, possibly to Iran; group maintained armed camps in Iraq under Saddam Hussein and is listed by US as terrorist organization, but has strong supporters in Pentagon who see it as pressure point on Iranian government; Iran has been trying to persuade Bush administration to hand over group and may move to expel members of Al Qaeda that it has in custody, although Bush administration rejects such exchange; none of group's members have been detained by US and they have been permitted to keep small weapons (M)

2003-12-13.title: General Says Cash and Arms Are Cut Off in Iraqi Hotbed
2003-12-13.abstract: Maj Gen Raymond T Odierno, who commands operations in Saddam Hussein's home city of Tikrit, says American forces have dealt damaging blow to insurgency in pivotal region in past month by choking off much of guerrillas' financing and arms supplies; acknowledges that there are still challenges from suicide bombings; says Americans have captured several important insurgent financiers and seized money stashed across region to pay for weapons and attacks, including nearly $2 million in American currency in Samarra; photo (M)

2003-12-14.title: Afghan Native Son, Home From America, Gives Orders
2003-12-14.abstract: US seeks to persuade Afghan emigres to return to their homeland and play leading role in revitalizing country's weak central government; is providing millions of dollars to augment government salaries to lure skilled Afghans home; Ali Ahmad Jalali, 62-year-old former Afghan Army colonel and military scholar, who lived in Washington for 20 years, has now returned to Afghanistan to become interior minister and one of country's most powerful men; photos (L)

2003-12-14.title: Ex-Leader, Found Hiding in Hole, Is Detained Without a Fight
2003-12-14.abstract: Coalition troops discovered Saddam Hussein hiding in a hole below a farm house located in Ad Dwar, 10 miles from Tikrit.

2003-12-14.title: In Blairs Words: Let His Capture Bring about Unity
2003-12-14.abstract: The following is the text of remarks made by Prime Minister Tony Blair following the capture of Saddam Hussein.

2003-12-14.title: Italian Debate on Fertility Bill Raises Issue of Church Influence
2003-12-14.abstract: Italian Senate's approval of bill drastically reducing fertility treatments is freighted with issues of enormous importance to nation's identity, like size of its future population and relationship between secularization and beliefs of Roman Catholic Church; country has second-lowest fertility rate in Europe, after Spain, and highest proportion of elderly people; government has introduced 1,000-euro payment for every child delivered before end of 2004, but new legislation blocks many routes toward pregnancy (S)

2003-12-14.title: President Bush Sees a Hopeful Day for Iraqi People
2003-12-14.abstract: The president told Iraqis that they need not fear the return of Saddam Hussein and pledged that he will face justice.

2003-12-15.title: 'We Got Him,' and Then a Call by American and Iraqi Officials for Reconciliation
2003-12-15.abstract: Excerpts from news conference following capture of Saddam Hussein; included are remarks by United States administrator L Paul Bremer III, Lt Gen Ricardo Sanchez, and Adnan Pachachi, acting president of Iraqi Governing Council; photo (M)

2003-12-15.title: Killed in Iraq
2003-12-15.abstract: *Defense Department confirms deaths of two American service members in Iraq, bringing total to 452 since start of war (S)

2003-12-15.title: For Candidates, a Day to Celebrate First and Worry Second
2003-12-15.abstract: Impact of Saddam Hussein's capture on Democratic presidential hopefuls assessed; Howard Dean, leading contender for nomination, has focused his candidacy around opposition to Iraq war; Sen Joseph Lieberman uses capture to reiterate his support for war and Sen John Kerry emphasizes need for leader with more foreign policy experience (M)

2003-12-15.title: U.S. Hopes Husseins Capture Will Bolster Support in U.N.
2003-12-15.abstract: The U.S. ambassador said today that he hoped the capture of Saddam Hussein would spur member states to close ranks behind U.S. plans.

2003-12-15.title: Arrest by U.S. Soldiers — President Still Cautious
2003-12-15.abstract: News of Saddam Husseins arrest, announced Sunday after being kept secret for 18 hours, electrified Iraq and much of the world.

2003-12-16.title: Bush Leaves Unclear Role of Iraqis In Any Trial
2003-12-16.abstract: Pres Bush, while vowing that Saddam Hussein's trial will be fair and open and involve Iraqis, leaves unresolved his willingness to back Iraqi Governing Council plan to try Hussein for war crimes, crimes against humanity and genocide; does not mention council's plan, news conference; experts comment (M)

2003-12-16.title: U.S. Officers Display the 'Rathole' Where Hussein Hid
2003-12-16.abstract: Saddam Hussein, after life of luxury, was captured in small, dark and dank hole in ground beneath courtyard of peasant farm; Col James B Hickey describes how soldiers peering down into shaft found him unarmed, apparently pleading for his life; photo (M)

2003-12-16.title: Israel Devised Then Dropped Plot to Kill Hussein, Reports Say
2003-12-16.abstract: Israel developed a risky plan in 1992 to assassinate Saddam Hussein at a funeral but shelved it after five Israeli soldiers were killed while training for the mission, according to news reports today.

2003-12-16.title: U.S. Troops Are Expected to Remain in Iraq at Least a 'Couple More Years,' Commander Says
2003-12-16.abstract: Lt Gen Ricardo Sanchez, commander of allied forces in Iraq, says troops will have to remain in Iraq for at least 'couple more years' to secure nation's stability and protect its borders against attack, interview before capture of Saddam Hussein; says United States is moving aggressively to try to hand over responsibility to Iraqi security forces and build their capacity; photo (M)

2003-12-16.title: World Briefing | Europe: Europe-Africa Tunnel
2003-12-16.abstract: Spain and Morocco begin research and testing on plan for 24-mile tunnel that would connect Europe and Africa; Spanish Public Works Minister Francisco Alvarez-Cascos estimates nations will spend $33 million over next three years in preparation and begin tunnel construction in 2008 (S)

2003-12-17.title: Pity at the Vatican for a Captive
2003-12-17.abstract: Cardinal Renato Martino, senior Vatican official, hails arrest of Saddam Hussein and expresses compassion for him, news conference; reproaches United States for releasing video images of him having teeth checked; reiterates Roman Catholic Church's opposition to death penalty; says Hussein should stand trial in appropriate place; photo (M)

2003-12-17.title: World Briefing | Africa: Ethiopia: After 9 Years, The Defense
2003-12-17.abstract: Trial of former Ethiopian leader Mengistu Haile Mariam, who is in exile in Zimbabwe, enters defense phase after nine years; he is being tried in absentia for his involvement in Red Terror, mass murders (S)

2003-12-17.title: Milosevic Trial Helps Clark Try To Gain Notice
2003-12-17.abstract: Gen Wesley K Clark, former NATO supreme commander and candidate for Democratic presidential nomination, seeks to burnish own credentials as potential commander in chief as he testifies at former Yugoslav Pres Slobodan Milosevic's war crimes trial, Hague; has maximized media value of his court appearance and official break from campaigning; comments on having faced down and testified against dictator, interview; ex-Pres Bill Clinton reportedly sends letter to court lauding Clark's role in halting ethnic cleansing in Kosovo after Milosevic reportedly attacked Clark by citing former Joint Chiefs of Staff chairman Gen Hugh Shelton's stand that Clark was asked to resign early from NATO post because of 'integrity and character issues' (M)

2003-12-17.title: Taiwan Reports SARS Case
2003-12-17.abstract: Taiwan health officials say medical researcher has contracted severe acute respiratory syndrome, apparently while working with virus in military laboratory; says risk of further cases is 'not high;' urge everyone on same flights researcher took on Dec 5 and 7 to consult doctor if they feel unwell (S)

2003-12-17.title: Link Between Afghanistan's North and South Is Restored
2003-12-17.abstract: Bush administration meets key goal in effort to secure and rebuild Afghanistan, completing reconstruction of road between Kabul and Kandahar; Amb Zalmay Khalilzad cites road's importance, dedication rite; United States provided $190 million to complete project, first phase of effort to rebuild roads that link Afghan cities; road's dedication is marred by security concerns; map; photo (M)

2003-12-18.title: Serbs May Help Patrol Afghanistan, but Qualms Abound
2003-12-18.abstract: Serbia's offer to send troop and police contingent to work alongside NATO in Afghanistan, and US willingness to consider offer, reflects great change in international situation since Balkan conflicts of 1990's; veteran human rights lawyer Natasa Kandic says Serb security forces should not go anywhere until they are properly reformed and senior commanders charged with war crimes are tried; photo (M)

2003-12-18.title: Annan Wants to Meet With Iraqi Council and U.S. Coalition
2003-12-18.abstract: Secretary General Kofi Annan said today that he was seeking a meeting next month to see if the U.N.s return to Baghdad could be accelerated.

2003-12-18.title: 6 From Greek Terror Group Get Life Sentences
2003-12-18.abstract: Greek court sentences leader of November 17 terrorist group, Alexandros Yiotopoulos, and five associates to multiple life sentences for string of killings, rocket attacks, bombings and bank robberies since 1975; Diitris Koufodinas gets 13 life terms for gunning down several of group's 23 victims, including British Brig Stephen Saunders (S)

2003-12-18.title: World Briefing | Europe: Northern Ireland: Premiers In Talks
2003-12-18.abstract: British Prime Minister Tony Blair meets with Irish Prime Minister Bertie Ahern and other leading political figures involved in power-sharing Northern Irish Assembly, in hopes of evaluating stalled political situation and determining path to resolution (S)

2003-12-18.title: Case of Ill Researcher Underlines Dangers in SARS Laboratories
2003-12-18.abstract: WHO says medical researcher ill with SARS in Taiwan appears to be isolated case involving person who worked with virus in medical laboratory there (M)

2003-12-19.title: World Briefing | Europe: Britain: Date Set For Diana Inquest
2003-12-19.abstract: British authorities announce plans for official inquest into death of Diana, Princess of Wales; inquest into circumstances of 1997 Paris car accident were delayed due to lengthy police investigation in France; separate inquest into death of Diana's companion Dodi Al Fayed will be held in Surrey (S)

2003-12-19.title: World Briefing: Europe
2003-12-19.abstract: LITHUANIA: President Faces Impeachment; BRITAIN: Date Set For Diana Inquest; SPAIN: A Law To Thwart A Basque Plan; FRANCE: Diplomats In Second Strike; FRANCE: Moody Toy Outlawed;.

2003-12-19.title: World Briefing | Middle East: Iran: Nuclear Inspections Signed
2003-12-19.abstract: Islamic Republic News Agency reports that Iran has signed agreement with United Nations International Atomic Energy Agency to allow surprise inspections at nuclear sites (S)

2003-12-19.title: U.S. Negotiating Over Role Of G.I.'s in a Sovereign Iraq
2003-12-19.abstract: Bush administration begins delicate negotiations with Iraqi transitional leaders on freedom US forces will have to carry out operations against insurgents after transfer of sovereignty June 30 (M)

2003-12-19.title: Robert Stanfield, 89, a Tory Who Lost Twice to Trudeau

2003-12-20.title: Sharon's New Offer: A Compromise, a Threat or Both?
2003-12-20.abstract: Richard Bernstein analysis contends that Israeli Prime Min Ariel Sharon's Dec 18 speech had two essential elements; says Sharon pledged to work hard to carry out US-supported peace plan, but warned that if there is no progress toward negotiated peace in next few months, Israel will move unilaterally to separate Jewish and Arab populations in West Bank

2003-12-20.title: New Prime Minister Is Steering Canada Cautiously to the Right
2003-12-20.abstract: Canadian Prime Min Paul Martin's first week in office has been marked by symbolic shifts in style and policy; trend in Ottawa is more businesslike and conservative, friendlier to US and to Canadian military; Martin is carefully leaning to right as he assembles his government and prepares for national elections in 2004 (M)

2003-12-20.title: World Briefing: Americas
2003-12-20.abstract: ARGENTINA: Ex-President Charged; VENEZUELA: Recall Petitions Filed;.

2003-12-20.title: World Briefing | Americas: Argentina: Ex-President Charged
2003-12-20.abstract: Argentinian court charges former President Carlos Saul Menem with tax fraud for failing to declare Swiss bank account holdings of $600,000; conviction would bar him from ever holding public office, but would not likely involve prison sentence; Justice Minister Gustavo Beliz holds Menem has at least two more Swiss accounts (S)

2003-12-20.title: 2 Kurdish Parties Close to Forming Unity Government
2003-12-20.abstract: Kurdish officials say Kurdish Democratic Party and Patriotic Union of Kurdistan, two governing parties in Iraq's long-divided Kurdish region, are close to establishing unified government; photo of Kurds in Iraq celebrating capture of Saddam Hussein (M)

2003-12-21.title: Spanish Leader Pays a Visit To His Troops In South Iraq
2003-12-21.abstract: Prime Min Jose Maria Aznar of Spain briefly visits with 1,300 Spanish soldiers stationed in south of Iraq; his visit, kept secret, mirrors that of Pres Bush on Thanksgiving Day; Aznar has been under pressure in Spain because of his alliance with Bush and Prime Min Tony Blair of Britain (M)

2003-12-21.title: Libya's 'Brother Leader' Pulls Yet Another Rabbit Out of His Hat
2003-12-21.abstract: Arab analysts and Libyan exiles say Libya agreed to divest itself of unconventional weapons to ensure that Col Muammar el-Qaddafi's absolute grip is not weakened; say infamously eccentric leader hopes not to suffer fate similar to Saddam Hussein; analysts are not convinced that promised destruction of weapons will have any meaningful impact, saying more than 30 years of purging ranks of educated Libyans has left country without ability to build such complex weapons; analysts doubt Qaddafi could have bought weapons abroad and maintained them, given international sanctions; they see Libyan announcement as public relations gesture (M)

2003-12-21.title: Italy's Leader Vows to Save Big Dairy Company From Bankruptcy
2003-12-21.abstract: Prime Min Silvio Berlusconi says Italian government would not allow bankruptcy of Parmalat, giant Italian dairy foods group that has disclosed $5 billion accounting discrepancy, shocking financial markets; says government would strive to safeguard jobs, and to distinguish financial side from operational part; government officials say judicial investigation would be opened to examine possible charges of fraud and providing falsified information to auditors (M)

2003-12-21.title: U.S. Won't Lift Sanctions Until Libya Keeps Its Word
2003-12-21.abstract: Bush administration officials say United States would proceed cautiously in lifting sanctions on Libya despite its promise to dismantle its illegal weapons programs, in order to signal North Korea that it must end its nuclear program before any rewards could be given out; say it is important not to offer any rewards to Libya until it begins actually to dismantle its chemical, biological and nuclear weapons (M)

2003-12-21.title: New Network to Combat Organized Crime in Southeast Europe
2003-12-21.abstract: State prosecutors from 12 southeastern European countries set up group to cooperate on transborder crimes; new Southeast European Prosecutors Advisory Group includes Albania, Bosnia and Herzegovina, Bulgaria, Croatia, Greece, Hungary, Macedonia, Moldova, Romania, Serbia and Montenegro, Slovenia and Turkey; prosecutors will act in tandem with other regional law enforcement authorities to combat organized crime (M)

2003-12-22.title: Israeli Forces Arrest a Senior Hamas Official in the West Bank
2003-12-22.abstract: Israeli security forces arrest senior Hamas official Adnan Asfour during series of raids in Nablus and nearby West Bank refugee camp; more raids are expected in ongoing effort to wipe out Hamas, radical group responsible for suicide bombings; Palestinians say boy, 5, was shot during raid on refugee camp; Israeli Army says troops were defending themselves; raids come as negotiators meet to try to arrange meeting between Palestinian Prime Min Ahmed Qurei and Israeli Prime Min Ariel Sharon; photo (M)

2003-12-22.title: Bomb Kills 2 G.I.s and Translator in Baghdad
2003-12-22.abstract: The attack came hours after coalition forces captured an important military officer from the former government of Saddam Hussein.

2003-12-22.title: Election Win Seems Assured for Guinean Leader, but His Nation's Fate Is Far From Certain
2003-12-22.abstract: Voter turnout is light in Guinea for election that Pres Lansana Conte is widely expected to win; main opposition parties boycott vote; Conte's ill health has raised fears of instability after his departure; International Crisis Group warns of possibile future military coup and armed movements fed by experienced, unemployed veterans of West Africa's recent wars; photo (M)

2003-12-22.title: Military in Iraq Is Warned Of Attacks During Holidays
2003-12-22.abstract: United States military officials say fresh intelligence indicates that Iraqi insurgents may be planning new wave of violence timed to Christmas holiday, in part to avenge Saddam Hussein's capture; Gen Richard B Myers says over 200 suspected insurgents have been detained in military raids in recent days, many as result of data contained in documents seized in Hussein's capture (M)

2003-12-22.title: THE YEAR IN SPORTS: A dead heat leaves fans alive with anticipation
2003-12-22.abstract: PARIS—  Horse racing fans can agree on almost nothing, but few would dispute this: the sport's most exciting moment of 2003 surely was the dead heat between High Chaparral and Johar in the Breeders' Cup Turf.

2003-12-23.title: Afghan Delegates Favor a Presidential System
2003-12-23.abstract: Afghan grand council, or loya jirga, overwhelmingly opts for democratic system with strong president, favored by Pres Hamid Karzai; final draft of constitution will be put to vote of full assembly; approval is expected; fundamentalists are pushing for parliamentary system but those favoring presidential system say they have enough votes (M)

2003-12-23.title: World Briefing: Africa
2003-12-23.abstract: KENYA: Thinning Out Prisons; IVORY COAST: Rebels To Rejoin Government;.

2003-12-23.title: World Briefing: Europe
2003-12-23.abstract: FRANCE: Queen Mary 2 Sets Sail; LITHUANIA: Tropical Tide; SPAIN: Immigration Crackdown; TURKEY: Arrests In Sinking Of Boat;.

2003-12-23.title: Arabs Rough Up Egyptian Official at Jerusalem Mosque
2003-12-23.abstract: Islamic radicals heckle and assault Ahmed Maher, Egypt's foreign minister, at Al Aksa mosque in Jerusalem; Maher suffers no injuries, but episode reflects tensions surrounding efforts to restart peace talks, in which Egypt is taking leading role; Palestinian Authority denounces attack; in other attacks, Palestinians kill two Israeli Army officers and are then shot dead in Gaza (M)

2003-12-23.title: Chief Arms Inspector Is Set to Go to Libya
2003-12-23.abstract: Dr Mohamed ElBaradei, International Atomic Energy Agency, will go to Libya to begin inspections of nuclear weapons program; plans follow Libya's admission of past nuclear ambitions and agreement with US and Great Britain to dismantle unconventional weapons and submit to inspections (M)

2003-12-24.title: World Briefing: Europe
2003-12-24.abstract: CYPRUS: New Government Sought; SPAIN: Lucky Town;.

2003-12-24.title: While reshaping a nation, they may also help reshape the EU : Changing times for Poland's youth
2003-12-24.abstract: KRAKOW:—  They are restless, hardworking and, according to one Polish sociologist, the "best-educated generation in the 1,000 years of Polish history."

2003-12-24.title: Food Agency Cuts Zimbabwe Rations and Warns of Shortages
2003-12-24.abstract: United Nations World Food Program halves food ration for 2.6 million malnourished people in Zimbabwe, citing shortfall of international aid; aid officials say supplies of some staples will competely run out early in Jan; say shortages could soon extend to five other southern African countries, where combination of drought and insufficient donations threatens to worsen hunger problems; World Food Program is trying to feed 6.5 million hungry people in Zimbabwe, Malawi, Mozambique, Zambia, Lesotho and Swaziland (M)

2003-12-24.title: Once Skeptical, Briton Sees Iraqi Success
2003-12-24.abstract: Maj Gen Graeme Lamb of Britain, preparing to hand his command in Iraq to another British general, says Saddam Hussein's capture and other changes have fostered new confidence that American-led occupation force can eventually hand politically stable Iraq back to its people; criticizes Western press for coming to early conclusion that allied undertaking in Iraq would not succeed, and failing to adjust; says he was skeptical himself at first (M)

2003-12-24.title: Killed in Iraq
2003-12-24.abstract: Defense Dept confirms deaths of two service members in Iraq (S)

2003-12-25.title: 4 G.I.'s and 6 Iraqi Civilians Are Killed in Bomb Attacks
2003-12-25.abstract: Christmas morning begins with Iraqi guerrillas unleashing string of intense rocket and bomb attacks across country, killing at least four US soldiers and six Iraqi civilians and wounding dozens more; American soldiers attend Christmas services in some Baghdad churches, which shift midnight masses to daytime for security; photos(M)

2003-12-25.title: Poisonous Fumes From Gas Well Explosion Kill 191 in China
2003-12-25.abstract: Scores of others were injured after an explosion rocked a natural gas field in southwestern China and spread poisonous fumes into surrounding communities.

2003-12-25.title: Sunni Groups Form Council to Gain Input Into Iraqi Rule
2003-12-25.abstract: Meanwhile today, powerful explosions and gunfire could be heard in parts of southern Baghdad, as American soldiers continued to try to route guerilla fighters.

2003-12-25.title: Popes Christmas Day Message
2003-12-25.abstract: The Vaticans English translation of John Pauls "Urbi et Orbi" remarks.

2003-12-25.title: Norway's Rules of the Road: A Heavy Emphasis on Equality
2003-12-25.abstract: Norwegian Prime Min Kjell Magne Bondevik's new armored BMW limousine is 88 pounds over legal limit for country's roads, in country where rules are for everyone and outward luxuries are suspect; back seat will be replaced with lighter one to comply with law (S)

2003-12-26.title: Egyptian Political Trial Delayed For Second Time, Stirring Anger
2003-12-26.abstract: Egyptian court postpones for second time trial of 23 Egyptians and three British subjects accused of trying to overthrow government; defendants have been in detention awaiting trial for 20 months; lawyers and defendants attribute delay to attack on Foreign Min Ahmed Maher in Jerusalem by Islamic radicals; families of defendants deplore delay (M)

2003-12-26.title: Gas Well Explosion and Fumes Kill 191 in China
2003-12-26.abstract: Explosion rocks natural gas field in Chongqing in southwestern China, killing at least 191 people as poisonous fumes spread into surrounding communities; high death toll and long lag in reporting accurate information raise possibility that accident resulted from poor safety procedures or faulty rescue operations, which are endemic in China's fast-growing economy; accident occurred when drillers apparently punctured highly pressurized store of natural gas and hydrogen sulfide, which can be lethal when inhaled; natural gas is high priority industry for China because of its energy needs, and also because authorities are eager to replace coal-burning furnaces with cleaner natural gas generators to fight air pollution which has reached hazardous levels in many major Chinese cities; photos; map (M)

2003-12-26.title: Pope, in Christmas Message, Pleads for End to Terrorism and War
2003-12-26.abstract: Pope John Paul II uses Christmas message to plead for end to terrorism worldwide and bloodshed in Middle East (M)

2003-12-26.title: French-Born Arabs, Perpetually Foreign, Grow Bitter
2003-12-26.abstract: Growing gap between traditional French population and children of Muslim immigrants who came to France in 1960's and 1970's examined; young Muslims simmer with frustration, isolated and alienated in Arab ghettos in large cities, as well as in small communities like St Florentin; many complain of racism, while Christian counterparts fear religion they do not understand and complain that Muslims do not want to integrate; many young Muslims in St Florentin are unemployed or underemployed, working for low wages, resentful that better jobs seem to be reserved for ethnic Europeans; photos (M)

2003-12-26.title: Iraqi Insurgents Kill Four U.S. Soldiers
2003-12-26.abstract: Today was the bloodiest day of attacks on the American military since the capture of Saddam Hussein on Dec. 13.

2003-12-27.title: Bombing After Lull: Israel Still Believes the Worst Is Over
2003-12-27.abstract: Richard Bernstein analysis holds that after first suicide bomb attack in Israel in almost three months, senior Israeli officials are offering what might seem paradoxical assessment; they say that despite bombing, worst of terrorism is already over because Israel is winning its war against terrorists; photo of Israeli mourners at grave of one of four Israelis killed by Palestinian suicide bomber in Petah Tikva (M)

2003-12-27.title: World Briefing | Europe: Spain: Another Bomb Found
2003-12-27.abstract: Spanish police and paramilitary find several bombs they contend were planted by two suspected ETA Basque militants arrested on December 24; one bomb is found in Mardrid train station and one in Aragon beneath rail line; third bomb in Aragon explodes, but no one is injured (S)

2003-12-27.title: Despite Attempts, Beagle 2 Still Unheard From
2003-12-27.abstract: Fate of British Beagle 2 Mars lander remains unknown after powerful radio telescope fails to detect its tiny signal while additional sensors are readied to join search in coming days (M)

2003-12-27.title: 40,000 Chinese Evacuated From Explosion 'Death Zone'
2003-12-27.abstract: Rescue workers continue to search for survivors and more bodies after poisonous cloud from natural gas explosion left what state news media called 'death zone' in Gaoqiao, China; over 40,000 people have been evacuated from area in southwestern China; death toll remains at 191; rescue workers photo (M)

2003-12-27.title: Milosevic's Name on the Ballot Signals Serbian Nationalism
2003-12-27.abstract: Slobodan Milosevic, former Serbian president who is on trial on war crimes charges, is running in parliamentary elections; it is symbolic of Serbia's increasingly nationalistic mood that Milosevic and war crime suspects Vojislav Seselj, Nebojsa Pavkovic and Sreten Lukic are running for office; photo of Milosevic supporters

2003-12-28.title: A Fighter for Catalan Pride Leaves a Legacy of Nationalism
2003-12-28.abstract: Jordi Pujol has made his version of Catalan autonomy the way of life for the 6.5 million inhabitants of the wealthy region.

2003-12-28.title: With No Rent, Still an Uneasy Life
2003-12-28.abstract: Description of scene in an apartment of Abi Nuwas Street housing complex in Baghdad, now occupied by squatters; photos; drawing (S)

2003-12-28.title: In China, a Possible SARS Case Alters Few Routines
2003-12-28.abstract: Newspapers carried the story on front pages, yet very few people were wearing the surgical masks that were so common early this year.

2003-12-28.title: Below Market. River View. Many Rooms. Expected Term of Occupancy: Day-to-Day
2003-12-28.abstract: Squatters are occupying apartments and houses along Tigris River in Baghdad that belonged to former government officials who fled city after American-led forces ousted Saddam Hussein; many fear that new government or military will evict them from their plush new homes; American military officials say that although they have allowed squatters to remain in public buildings throughout city, Iraqi transitional government that will take power on July 1 will almost certainly want buildings back for their own use, or at least will want to charge rent; portraits of some squatters; photos (L)

2003-12-28.title: Israeli Protesters Wounding by Soldiers Prompts a Debate
2003-12-28.abstract: Almost everyone in Israel, from President Moshe Katsav on down has joined a national debate on why soldiers had used live ammunition to shoot an unarmed Israeli demonstrator.

2003-12-29.title: Killed in Iraq
2003-12-29.abstract: Defense Dept confirms deaths of four service members in Iraq, bringing total to 476 since beginning of war (S)

2003-12-29.title: Libyas Nuclear Program Mostly Dismantled, U.N. Inspector Says
2003-12-29.abstract: The United Nations top nuclear inspector said today that Libya was years away from producing a nuclear weapon.

2003-12-29.title: The Country Will Blossom
2003-12-29.abstract: Governing Council member Dara Nur al-Din discusses the trial of Saddam Hussein before a war crimes tribunal and the future of Baghdad following the downfall of the dictator.

2003-12-29.title: Journalist fired for book critical of French newspapers : Author sees anti-U.S. reporting
2003-12-29.abstract: PARIS:—  Alain Hertoghe believes that in covering the Iraq conflict, French newspapers recreated "the war they would have liked to have seen." That meant concentration on the Vietnams and Stalingrads that didn't take place, he said, and so many more accounts of U.S. difficulties rather than advances that it was "impossible to understand how the Americans won."

2003-12-29.title: Serb Rightists Are Big Winners, but Not Big Enough to Rule
2003-12-29.abstract: Ultranationalists win largest number of seats in parliamentary elections in Belgrade, Serbia; party is headed by Vojislav Seselj, who is currently on trial for war crimes at international criminal tribunal in The Hague; Slobadan Milosevic, another war crimes defendant, also wins election; Vojislav Kostinuca, whose party trails Seselj's party, is said to be favorite to lead new administration (M)

2003-12-30.title: Afghans Clash at a Conference To Work Out a New Constitution
2003-12-30.abstract: Delegates at two-week-long constitutional conference in Kabul accuse chairman of allowing government interference in final drafting, while ignoring views of elected representatives; organizers and members of government have feared that religious conservatives and leaders of former mujahedeen armies will hijack loya jirga by sheer force of numbers and their intimidating presence; it is not clear how far they will take their protest; photo (M)

2003-12-30.title: Musharraf's Quandary
2003-12-30.abstract: If Islamic extremists were behind the two attempted assassinations of President Pervez Musharraf of Pakistan, he may be forced to reconsider his simultaneous support for the United States and his reluctance to confront the militants more directly. But cracking down on the militants would have political costs.      Article, Page A3.

2003-12-30.title: Iraqi Civilian Killed by Bomb Apparently Aimed at G.I.s
2003-12-30.abstract: An Iraqi civilian was killed and three others were wounded when a bomb apparently aimed at U.S. soldiers exploded in Baghdad.

2003-12-30.title: Putin and His Political Allies Extend Power in Parliament
2003-12-30.abstract: Newly elected Russian Parliament convenes and bloc supporting Pres Vladimir V Putin establishes two-thirds majority, which will allow it to pass any laws or make any constitutional changes it wishes; Putin is expected to be re-elected in March, and rumor is that he could amend Constitution to allow third term after that; independent member of Parliament compares present situation with time of czars; photo (M)

2003-12-30.title: LIBYA'S ATOM BID IN EARLY PHASES
2003-12-30.abstract: Top United Nations nuclear inspector Dr Mohamed ElBaradei says Libya's nuclear program is years away from producing nuclear weapons and that important pieces of equipment are now largely dismantled and stored in boxes; comments after visiting four previously undeclared sites in Libya where scientists had been working to perfect enrichment of uranium; he expresses surprise that Libya had acquired great deal of high-technology equipment needed to enrich uranium through black-market transactions that have yet to be disclosed; says existence of shadowy network of middlemen who circumvent national export controls proves that those controls are not working; ElBaradei, head of International Atomic Energy Agency, meets with Col Muammar el-Qaddafi; Libyan leader reiterates his pledge to rid country of illicit weapons; he has also pledged to begin immediate compliance with Nuclear Nonproliferation Treaty, especially by submitting to unannounced inspections of any site; photo (M)

2003-12-31.title: Blast Kills 5 at Restaurant as Rash of Bombings Hit Iraq
2003-12-31.abstract: Though authorities said the cause of the blast remained unclear, one witness said he saw a car swing into an alley next to the restaurant just before the explosion.

2003-12-31.title: World Briefing | Europe: Lithuania: Court Says President Violated Constitution
2003-12-31.abstract: Lithuanian Constitutional Court rules that President Rolandas Paksas violated three articles of Constitution when he granted citizenship to Yuri Borisov, Russian arms dealer, thought to have ties to organized crime; Parliament has begun impeachment proceedings against Paksas (S)

2003-12-31.title: Philippines to Deport U.S. Brothers Held in Terror Inquiry
2003-12-31.abstract: Philippines will deport Michael Ray and Jamil Daoud Mujahid, American brothers suspected of terrorist links; photo; Stubbs worked for Lawrence Livermore National Laboratory until 2000; Filipino authorities say Mujahid, convert to Islam, met with members of extremist groups Abu Sayyaf and Moro Islamic Liberation Front (M)

2003-12-31.title: Russia and the Rich Western Neighbors: A Cold Peace
2003-12-31.abstract: Letter from Europe on Russia's traditional ambivalence about being part of West, at time when European Union's plan to envelop former Soviet satellites leaves Russia on other side of elite club of democratic states; Russian consumers with money to spend delight in Western Europe's glitter, but money and goods have turned Moscow and other cities into oases of consumption in country still beset by poverty; photo; Pres Vladimir Putin's increasingly authoritarian, and popular, rule also reflects disenchantment with decade of democratic experiment; Putin himself says it will be up to new generation to see how relationship with European Union develops (M)

2003-12-31.title: Israel Says Settlement Population Has Doubled Since '93
2003-12-31.abstract: Israel reports that number of Jewish settlers in West Bank and Gaza increased 16 percent in last three years, to 236,381, about double number in 1993 when Oslo Accords were signed; Israeli helicopter fies missiles at car carrying senior Hamas figures and soldiers at Gaza checkpoint kill Palestinian suspected of planting bomb (S)
